Short title and commencement :- (1) These rules may be called the coinage of One Hundred Rupees and Five Rupees coms to commemorate the occasion of “150 YEARS OF KUKA MOVEMENT” Rules, 2012. (2) They shall come into force on the'date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. The coins of the following denominations shall be coined at the Mint for issue under the authority of the Central Government to commemorate the occasion of “150 YEARS OF KUKA MOVEMENT", namely:— (a) One Hundred Rupees; (b) Five Rupees. 3. The coins of the denominations referred to in rule 3, shall conform to the following dimensions, designs and composition, namely:— Denomlnation Shape and Number of Metal Composition of the coin outside Serrations diameter (1), “22(3) . (3) 1.4L ,, fl ,, One Hundred Circular ' 200 Quaternary Alloy Rupees 44 millimeters Silver — 50 percent Copper— 40 percent Nickel — 5 percent Zinc — 5 percent Circular 100 Nickel Brass 23 millimeters Copper — 75 percent Zinc ~ 20 percent Nickel — 5 percent WFive RuEeE' -- 4 of 6 -- I'jllTl II we 3(i)| “NW 317 W : aHlUl’Ull DESIGN 1. ONE HUNDRED RUPEES / OBVERSE The face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar in the centre with the legend “WENT-T ” inscribed below, flanked on‘the left periphery with the word “W in Devnagri script and on the right periphery flanked with the word ”INDIA" in English. It shall also bear the Rupee symbol “ T and denominational value “100" in International Numerals below the Lion Capitol. REVERSE The face of the coin shall bear the combined portrait of Satguru Ram Singh Jl in the centre, his followers hanged on the left side and on the right side standing in front of the mouth of canon, with inscription “ WWWQKHOHTT“ in Devnagri script on the outer left periphery and “ 150 YEARS OF KUKA MOVEMENT" in English on the outer right periphery. The inscription “ FEW}? TNT a? Sh ” in Devnagri script shall be shown above the portrait on the left inner periphery and “SATGURU RAM SINGH Jl" in English shall be flanked on the right inner periphery. The year‘s figure “1857—2007” shall be shown below the portrait in lnternational Numerals. 2. FIVE RUPEES OBVERSE The face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar in the centre with the legend “WENT-T " inscribed below, flanked on the left periphery with the word “SlllEl‘ in Devnagri script and on the right periphery flanked with the word ”INDIA” in English. It shall also bear the Rupee symbol “T‘ and denominational value “5” in International Numerals below the Lion Capitol. REVERSE The face of the coin shall bear the combined portrait of Satguru Ram Singh Ji in the centre, his followers hanged on the left side and on the right side standing in front of the mouth of canon. with inscription “ W WW 3? 150 a“? “ in Devnagri script on the outer left periphery and “ 150 YEARS OF KUKA MOVEMENT" in English on the outer right periphery The inscription “ WT}? TNT f3? Sh " in Devnagri script shall be shown above the portrait on the left inner periphery and “SATGURU RAM SINGH Jl” in English shall be flanked on the right inner periphery. The year‘s figure ”1857—2007" shall be shown below the portrait in international Numerals 3.18) GI]22«2 -- 5 of 6 -- 6 H H”, CAKE!" ‘3 4.
